overview_purpose_1.description: Explains pytest's purpose as a Python testing framework, its public entry points, documentation shape, and the high-level path from CLI invocation through configuration, session setup, collection, and test execution.

setup_development_1.description: Explains how pytest is installed, how contributors prepare a local checkout, and how tox, pre-commit, documentation builds, changelog entries, and release tooling fit into the development workflow.

public_api_interface_1.description: Explains how pytest's top-level public API is assembled, where the reference documentation points users, and how key exported helpers for comparisons, exceptions, outcomes, and warnings behave.

config_plugins_hooks_1.description: Explains how pytest builds configuration, discovers and controls plugins, loads conftest files, and exposes hook-based extension points during startup and test execution.

collection_nodes_1.description: Explains how pytest turns command-line inputs into a collection tree, how collection nodes are constructed and identified, and how filesystem and Python collectors produce runnable test items.

fixtures_parametrize_marks_1.description: Explains how pytest turns fixture parameters and parametrize marks into collected test cases, how per-parameter marks are stored on items, and how skip, xfail, and marker selection consume those marks.

assertions_warnings_outcomes_1.description: Explains how pytest turns plain asserts into useful failure reports, how warning capture and warning assertions work, and how skip, fail, xfail, exit, raises, and import-based outcomes are represented.

runtime_reporting_io_1.description: Explains how pytest runs test phases, turns phase results into reports, attaches captured output and logs, prints terminal progress and summaries, and manages tmp_path directories.
